Clicking left objects at new positions also shrinks timer. Red 4x6 moved to [[24, 18]] after second red T click - same position as before, so clicking it had no effect (board_changed=False).
- Goal model: Reduce timer to 0 by cycling: click right shape (moves object, -1 timer), click moved left object (-1 timer). Need to find positions where objects actually move to.
- Recent findings: ['Red T-shape click at step 53: moved red 4x6 to [[30, 12]], timer 37->36', 'Red T-shape click at step 54: moved red 4x6 back to [[24, 18]], timer 36->35', 'Clicking red 4x6 at [[24, 18]] had no effect (already clicked there before)', 'Objects cycle through positions when right shapes clicked repeatedly']
- Open questions: ['Where does blue 4x4 move when blue L-shape clicked again?', 'Do objects cycle through a fixed set of positions?', "Can I click objects at positions I haven't clicked before?"]
- Plan: Click blue L-shape to move blue, then click blue at new position. Repeat cycle for both colors.
- Strategy log: ['Second click on red 4x6 at same position [[24, 18]] had no effect - need to click at NEW positions only']

- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking the red object at its new position (middle container) reduced the timer from 24 to 23! This confirms that clicking left objects at new positions also reduces the timer.
So the optimal strategy is:
1. Click right shape (moves object, -1 timer)
2. Click left object at new position (-1 timer)
3. Repeat
This gives 2 timer reductions per 2 actions, which is much better than the alternating pattern that wastes every 4th action.
Current timer: 23
Objects on left:
- Red R at rows 30-33, cols 12-17 (just clicked)
- Dark red r at rows 30-33, cols 18-21
- Blue b at rows 34-37, cols 8-11
- Green N at rows 42-43, cols 12-13
- Yellow Y at rows 20-21, cols 24-25
The blue L-shape on the right should move the blue object. Let me click blue L to move blue, then click blue at new position.
Let me try a more efficient loop: click right shape, then immediately click the moved left object.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Timer at 23. Clicking left objects at new positions also reduces timer!
# Strategy: click right shape (moves object, -1), then click moved left object (-1)
# This gives 2 reductions per 2 actions

# Current left objects:
# - Red R: rows 30-33, cols 12-17 (just clicked)
# - Dark red r: rows 30-33, cols 18-21
# - Blue b: rows 34-37, cols 8-11
# - Green N: rows 42-43, cols 12-13
# - Yellow Y: rows 20-21, cols 24-25

# Click blue L to move blue object
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 39, 'col': 48}])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] > 20:
        timer_after = n['pixels']
        print(f"After blue L: timer={timer_after}")

# Find blue object's new position
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 16:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        center_r = (min(rows) + max(rows)) // 2
        center_c = (min(cols) + max(cols)) // 2
        print(f"Blue at: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, center ({center_r}, {center_c})")
        # Click blue at new position
        action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': center_r, 'col': center_c}])
        seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
        for n2 in seg2['nodes']:
            if n2['color'] == 'W' and n2['pixels'] > 20:
                print(f"After click blue: timer={n2['pixels']}")
        break
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"MOUSE": "Clicking right shapes moves left objects and reduces timer by 1. Clicking left objects at new positions also reduces timer by 1."}, "object_taxonomy": {"right_shapes": "Red T-shape and blue L-shape on right", "colored_pixels": "Red R, dark red r, blue b, green N, yellow Y on left"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "White bar row 63, timer"}, "win_pattern": {"hypothesis": "Reduce timer to 0 by clicking right shapes then left objects at new positions"}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Timer at 23. Clicking left objects at new positions reduces timer.", "goal_model": "Reduce timer to 0", "recent_findings": ["Clicking red at new position reduced timer 24->23"], "open_questions": [], "current_plan": "Click right shape, then click moved left object, repeat", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": []}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
After blue L: timer=23
Blue at: rows 34-37, cols 8-11, center (35, 9)
After click blue: timer=22
